statement.focus_question: Is the minimum density achieved when all the $a_i$ are equal?
statement.normalized: Let $A=\{n_1<\cdots<n_r\}$ be a finite set of positive integers. What is the maximum density of integers covered by a suitable choice of congruences $a_i\pmod{n_i}$? Is the minimum density achieved when all the $a_i$ are equal?
statement.original:
Let $A=\{n_1<\cdots<n_r\}$ be a finite set of positive integers. What is the maximum density of integers covered by a suitable choice of congruences $a_i\pmod{n_i}$?

Is the minimum density achieved when all the $a_i$ are equal?
